data.title: Web UI v1: two-pane workbench (chat + run inspector), dark cockpit default + light toggle

data.body: Decided with David 2026-06-11 in conversation (decisions are final): proper web app, NOT the minimal FastAPI-served HTML page. Stack: Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui, static SPA built to web/dist, served by FastAPI (NAVI-16) -- same-origin, no CORS, keeps localhost-only security posture. Next.js explicitly rejected (no SSR need). VISUAL: 'Jarvis dark cockpit' as DEFAULT theme (dark slate bg, cyan/teal accents, monospace data readouts, glowing status dots) + light 'clean workbench' theme toggle (neutral grays, single accent). V1 SCOPE (chat + run inspector ONLY): two-pane layout -- LEFT: conversation thread (route badge per answer: answer_inline/research/sap_review/clarify/refuse each distinct color; evidence chip expanding cited sources; per-run cost) + input box hitting POST /ask. RIGHT: Run Inspector -- trace timeline for selected run via GET /runs/{id}: route_decision, model_call (tokens), broker_decision verdicts as first-class UI (green check allowed / red X denied with reason / amber shield when output redacted, showing NAVI-14 labels), total tokens in/out + cost vs budget meter. Header: app title + /health status dots + theme toggle. V2 (separate future tasks, NOT v1): runs history table page, SAP review card rendering (Summary/Findings/Gaps/Quick wins), streaming. Depends on NAVI-16.

DESIGN REFINEMENT from David (2026-06-11, two reference images: a Lovable-built JARVIS voice UI + an n8n Jarvis Tools Agent workflow). Visual language for the dark default theme, supersedes the earlier cyan/teal note: (1) PALETTE deep blues/blacks (near-black navy base ~#0a0f1e) with ELECTRIC BLUE accents (~#2f81f7/#38bdf8); (2) GLASS MORPHISM panels -- backdrop-blur, translucent fills, light 1px border accents to separate UI elements; (3) HERO: animated glowing arc-reactor orb (concentric rings, HUD circuit lines) -- center stage on empty thread like a boot screen, shrinks into header once chatting; pulses idle, animates while a run is in flight, shifts color on deny/refuse; (4) HUD READOUTS: header '* ONLINE' dot from /health, small monospace 'SYS.STATUS' strip, 'NAVI V0.1 // ONLINE' wordmark, monospace numerals for tokens/cost; (5) typography Apple-grade (Inter or similar + mono for data), smooth message/loading transitions. Run Inspector styled as the HUD diagnostics panel (broker allowed/denied/redacted as glowing status glyphs). EXPLICITLY OUT for v1: microphone/voice input + ElevenLabs TTS (new scope, possible future task); any write-action agents from the n8n reference (Email/Calendar/Contact/Content) -- Navi is read-only by design, the broker is the differentiator and the UI should showcase it.

SCOPE ADDITION from David (2026-06-12): the n8n reference image was shown specifically for its WORKFLOW VISUALIZATION -- he wants a run-graph canvas view in Navi. Requirement: an n8n-style node graph of a run: POST /ask -> Router -> Model loop <-> Broker -> {knowledge_base_search, web_search} -> Response, with the selected run's trace events overlaid: nodes/edges light up as executed, broker verdicts on the tool edges (green=allowed, red=denied+glow, amber=redacted), edge labels with call counts / tokens / cost (like n8n's 'N items'). Navi's topology is FIXED (single agent, 2 tools), so implement as a custom SVG canvas with fixed layout -- NO graph library dependency (react-flow rejected as unnecessary for ~7 static nodes; revisit only if topology becomes dynamic). Becomes STEP 3 of this task, after step 2 (chat + timeline inspector); the inspector gets tabs: Timeline | Graph. Aesthetic: same Jarvis HUD language -- glass nodes, electric-blue circuit edges, glow states.

REVIEW (agent:claude-fable-5-reviewer, cold read of dev @60b55fe).

Per-area:
- App.tsx ask flow: PASS. void submit() is the correct floating-promise idiom (errors fully caught inside submit's try/finally; no white-screen path). Nested try/catch around getRun: 404 -> 'RUN NOT FOUND', other -> 'TRACE UNAVAILABLE', thread unaffected. orbState idle/active driven by pending; input + send disabled while pending. Network/ApiError on /ask -> in-thread red error bubble. No leaks.
- TraceEventRow: PASS (security-critical). Redaction detected as verdict==='allowed' && payload_hash!==null — matches backend trace.py:77-83 exactly. Glyphs correct: denied=red X, redacted=amber shield, allowed=green check. NO clear-text invented: payload_hash rendered ONLY as truncated mono '#xxxxxxxx…' with sha256 tooltip. Verified against trace.py: denied events DO carry payload_hash=_hash(reason) but verdict==='denied' guard correctly keeps them in the denied branch (not misread as redacted). No leak.
- RunGraph: PASS. Redaction rule identical to timeline (verdict==='allowed' && payload_hash!==null, line 47). Aggregates correct (modelCalls/tokens summed over model_call; per-tool allowed/denied/redacted). Fixed-layout SVG, viewBox 0 0 710 400, no graph lib. All colors via CSS vars (--navi-ok/warn/danger/glow/panel + shadcn tokens) — confirmed defined for BOTH :root(light) and .dark in index.css; --navi-glow collapses to transparent in light mode (no broken glow).
- useHealth: PASS. clearInterval(id) on unmount; cancelled flag guards all setState. No leak.
- ThemeProvider: PASS. dark is default (stored!=='light' -> dark); persists to localStorage 'navi.theme'; toggles .dark on documentElement.
- Message/EvidenceChip/ChatInput/InspectorPane: PASS. key props correct (msg.id, evidence src), truncated/needs_approval chips, ChatInput trims+guards disabled, tabs render from in-memory RunTrace (no refetch). AskRequest{text} matches client JSON.stringify({text}). All 4 contracts match contracts.py.

Finding (LOW, non-blocking, cosmetic): reserved 'approval_required' verdict renders amber in TraceEventRow but falls through to muted in RunGraph.toolColor (only denied/redacted/allowed handled). Plan marks this verdict as never-currently-emitted/reserved, so no live impact. Could add an approval branch to toolColor later.

No clear-text-where-only-hash-exists leak found. No correctness bugs.

VERDICT: PASS
